name: GaugeFlow P5-D0 single-endpoint unconditional coordinate-flow qualification v1
purpose: Qualify the coordinate-flow backbone on one fixed synthetic endpoint before any endpoint-ID or tensor-conditioned control. This protocol has no tensor condition, harmonic token/grid, null mask, CFG, endpoint ID, learned oracle, or real-material label.

parent_evidence.reason: A single endpoint removes conditional branch selection and tests whether the production coordinate path can fit and freely sample a fixed periodic geometry.

state.endpoint_source: P5 v1 endpoint index 0 (exact same four atom types, triclinic lattice, fractional coordinate target, and analytic teacher)
state.atom_types: fixed endpoint values; not an input condition
state.lattice: fixed endpoint value; not an input condition

teacher.formula: T(X)=sum_{i->j}(a_j-a_i) exp(-r_ij/delta) n_ij outer n_ij outer n_ij
teacher.symmetry: analytic periodic SO(3)-equivariant rank-three diagnostic; not a learned or real piezoelectric oracle
teacher.target_orbit_evaluation: fixed 240-node SO(3) evaluation panel only; it is not a harmonic-conditioning grid

failure_attribution.free_running_sampling_failure: teacher-forced fit passes but periodic-coordinate RMS, orbit error, retrieval, or sampling-failure criterion fails
failure_attribution.passed: every frozen criterion passes for every fixed seed

decision_rule: A pass permits one separately versioned P5-D1 endpoint-ID experiment. A failure prohibits P5-D1 and requires diagnosis of the unconditional coordinate flow/backbone or sampler. Neither outcome activates P3, P4, external oracles, real tensors, relaxation, DFT, or DFPT.
